# Chimichurri Steak with Roasted Potatoes and Simple Salad

Status: Stage 1 Draft  
Archive folder: `recipe-archive/beef/`  
Category: Beef  
Source or inspiration: Week 29 family menu idea  
Date added: 2026-07-04  

## Planning Summary

- Servings: 4
- Estimated prep time: 25 minutes
- Estimated cook time: 35 minutes
- Protein: Steak
- Cuisine or flavor direction: Argentinian-inspired grilled steak, parsley, cilantro, vinegar, garlic
- Best day to cook: Friday
- Perishability notes: Steak, fresh herbs, lettuce, and tomatoes should cook before the weekend.
- Difficulty: Signature

## Equipment

- Grill or grill pan
- Sheet pan
- Mixing bowl
- Cutting board and knife
- Instant-read thermometer

## Ingredients

Assume nothing is on hand. Include quantities for everything used.

| Quantity | Ingredient | Preferred version/type | Acceptable alternatives | Notes |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 2 lb | Flank steak or skirt steak | Grill-friendly cut | Sirloin steak | Main protein |
| 1 tbsp | Neutral oil | Avocado or canola oil | Vegetable oil | Steak |
| 1 1/2 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt, use less | Steak |
| 1 tsp | Black pepper | Freshly ground | Ground black pepper | Steak |
| 3 lb | Baby potatoes | Halved | Yukon gold potatoes, cubed | Side |
| 2 tbsp | Olive oil | Extra-virgin or regular | Avocado oil | Potatoes |
| 1 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Potatoes |
| 1/2 tsp | Black pepper | Ground | Freshly ground | Potatoes |
| 1 cup | Fresh parsley | Finely chopped | Cilantro | Chimichurri |
| 1/2 cup | Cilantro | Finely chopped | Extra parsley | Chimichurri |
| 4 cloves | Garlic | Fresh minced | 1 tsp garlic powder | Chimichurri |
| 1 | Jalapeno | Seeded and minced | Red pepper flakes | Chimichurri |
| 2 tbsp | Red wine vinegar | Red wine vinegar | Apple cider vinegar | Chimichurri |
| 2 tbsp | Lemon juice | Fresh | Bottled lemon juice | Chimichurri |
| 1/3 cup | Olive oil | Extra-virgin | Avocado oil | Chimichurri |
| 1 tsp | Dried oregano | Dried | 1 tbsp fresh oregano | Chimichurri |
| 1/2 tsp | Red pepper flakes | Mild | Skip for less heat | Chimichurri |
| 1/2 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Chimichurri |
| 1 heart | Romaine lettuce | Chopped | Green leaf lettuce | Salad |
| 1 pint | Grape tomatoes | Halved | Cherry tomatoes | Salad |
| 1/2 | Red onion | Thinly sliced | Shallot | Salad |
| 1 tbsp | Olive oil | Extra-virgin or regular | Avocado oil | Salad |
| 1 tbsp | Lemon juice | Fresh | Lime juice | Salad |
| 1/4 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Salad |

## Basic Instructions

1. Heat oven to 425 degrees F. Toss potatoes with olive oil, salt, and pepper. Roast 30 to 35 minutes, turning once, until browned and tender.
2. Mix parsley, cilantro, garlic, jalapeno, red wine vinegar, lemon juice, olive oil, oregano, red pepper flakes, and salt. Let chimichurri sit while the steak cooks.
3. Pat steak dry. Rub with oil, salt, and pepper.
4. Grill over medium-high heat until browned and cooked to preferred doneness, about 4 to 6 minutes per side for flank steak. Aim for 130 to 135 degrees F for medium-rare or 140 to 145 degrees F for medium.
5. Rest steak 10 minutes, then slice thinly against the grain.
6. Toss romaine, tomatoes, and red onion with olive oil, lemon juice, and salt.
7. Serve sliced steak with chimichurri, roasted potatoes, and salad.

## Notes

- What might need testing: Chimichurri acidity and heat level.
- Possible substitutions: Sirloin steak works if flank or skirt is unavailable.
- Prep-ahead ideas: Chop chimichurri herbs Friday morning. Roast potatoes day-of for crisp edges.
- Family preference concerns: Slice against the grain or flank steak can feel chewy.

# Moroccan Meatballs with Lemon Yogurt Sauce

Status: Stage 1 Draft  
Archive folder: `recipe-archive/beef/`  
Category: Beef  
Source or inspiration: Previous family draft recipe  
Date added: 2026-07-04  

## Planning Summary

- Servings: 4 to 6
- Estimated prep time: 20 minutes
- Estimated cook time: 20 minutes
- Protein: Ground beef
- Cuisine or flavor direction: Moroccan-inspired, warm spices, lemon yogurt sauce
- Best day to cook: Early week
- Perishability notes: Uses ground beef, yogurt, lemon, and parsley. Cook within 1 to 2 days of shopping.

## Equipment

- Large mixing bowl
- Large skillet or sheet pan
- Small bowl
- Cutting board and knife
- Instant-read thermometer

## Ingredients

Assume nothing is on hand. Include quantities for everything used.

| Quantity | Ingredient | Preferred version/type | Acceptable alternatives | Notes |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 2 lb | Ground beef | 85/15 or 90/10 | Ground turkey or lamb | Main protein |
| 1 large | Egg | Large | 2 tbsp Greek yogurt | Binder |
| 1/2 cup | Breadcrumbs | Plain | Panko breadcrumbs | Binder |
| 4 cloves | Garlic | Fresh minced | 1 tsp garlic powder | Meatballs and sauce |
| 1 tsp | Ground cumin | Ground | None | Meatballs |
| 1 tsp | Paprika | Sweet or smoked | Smoked paprika | Meatballs |
| 1 tsp | Ground coriander | Ground | Skip if unavailable | Meatballs |
| 1/2 tsp | Ground cinnamon | Ground | Skip for milder flavor | Meatballs |
| 1 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt, use less | Meatballs |
| 1/2 tsp | Black pepper | Ground | Freshly ground | Meatballs |
| 2 tbsp | Fresh parsley | Chopped | Cilantro | Meatballs |
| 1 tbsp | Neutral oil | Avocado or canola oil | Olive oil | For skillet cooking |
| 1 cup | Plain Greek yogurt | Full-fat or 2 percent | Plain yogurt, strained | Sauce |
| 1 | Lemon | Juiced | Bottled lemon juice | Sauce |
| 1/4 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Sauce |
| 1/4 tsp | Black pepper | Ground | Freshly ground | Sauce |
| 1 1/2 cups dry | Jasmine rice or couscous | Jasmine rice for bowls, couscous for faster meal | Long-grain white rice | Serving |

## Basic Instructions

1. Mix beef, egg, breadcrumbs, 3 minced garlic cloves, cumin, paprika, coriander, cinnamon, salt, pepper, and parsley until just combined.
2. Form 20 to 24 meatballs.
3. For skillet cooking, heat oil over medium-high heat and brown meatballs in batches. Lower heat, cover, and cook until the centers reach 160 degrees F.
4. For oven cooking, bake meatballs on a sheet pan at 400 degrees F for 18 to 20 minutes, until browned and cooked through.
5. Mix yogurt, lemon juice, 1 minced garlic clove, salt, and pepper in a small bowl.
6. Serve meatballs over rice or couscous with lemon yogurt sauce.

## Notes

- What might need testing: Cinnamon level and whether the sauce needs more lemon.
- Possible substitutions: Ground lamb can make this richer; ground turkey may need 1 tbsp olive oil in the mixture.
- Prep-ahead ideas: Shape meatballs up to 24 hours ahead and refrigerate covered.
- Family preference concerns: Keep sauce on the side for anyone sensitive to tangy yogurt sauces.

# Orange Beef with Jasmine Rice and Broccoli

Status: Stage 1 Draft  
Archive folder: `recipe-archive/beef/`  
Category: Beef  
Source or inspiration: Week 29 family menu idea  
Date added: 2026-07-04  

## Planning Summary

- Servings: 4
- Estimated prep time: 20 minutes
- Estimated cook time: 25 minutes
- Protein: Beef
- Cuisine or flavor direction: Chinese-American citrus takeout-inspired, orange, ginger, garlic
- Best day to cook: Monday
- Perishability notes: Fresh beef, oranges, and broccoli are best early in the week.
- Difficulty: Easy

## Equipment

- Large skillet or wok
- Medium saucepan with lid
- Mixing bowls
- Microplane or zester
- Cutting board and knife

## Ingredients

Assume nothing is on hand. Include quantities for everything used.

| Quantity | Ingredient | Preferred version/type | Acceptable alternatives | Notes |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1 1/2 lb | Flank steak | Thinly sliced against the grain | Sirloin or skirt steak | Main protein |
| 1/4 cup | Arrowroot powder | Arrowroot starch | Tapioca starch | Coats beef |
| 3 tbsp | Neutral oil | Avocado or canola oil | Vegetable oil | Searing |
| 1 1/2 lb | Broccoli florets | Fresh | Frozen broccoli florets | Side |
| 1 tbsp | Neutral oil | Avocado or canola oil | Vegetable oil | Broccoli |
| 1/4 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Broccoli |
| 1 1/2 cups | Jasmine rice | Dry | Long-grain white rice | Side |
| 2 1/4 cups | Water | Cold water | Low-sodium broth | Rice |
| 1/2 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Rice |
| 1/2 cup | Orange juice | Fresh squeezed | Bottled orange juice | Sauce |
| 1 tbsp | Orange zest | Fresh | Skip if using bottled juice | Sauce |
| 1/3 cup | Low-sodium soy sauce | Low-sodium | Tamari | Sauce |
| 2 tbsp | Rice vinegar | Unseasoned | White vinegar | Sauce |
| 3 tbsp | Brown sugar | Light brown sugar | Honey | Sauce |
| 4 cloves | Garlic | Fresh minced | 1 tsp garlic powder | Sauce |
| 1 tbsp | Fresh ginger | Grated | 1 tsp ground ginger | Sauce |
| 1/4 tsp | Red pepper flakes | Mild | Skip for less heat | Optional |
| 4 | Green onions | Sliced | Chives | Finish |

## Basic Instructions

1. Start rice: rinse jasmine rice, then simmer with water and salt for 15 minutes. Rest covered 10 minutes.
2. Whisk orange juice, orange zest, soy sauce, rice vinegar, brown sugar, garlic, ginger, and red pepper flakes.
3. Toss sliced beef with arrowroot until lightly coated.
4. Stir-fry broccoli in 1 tbsp oil with salt until bright green and crisp-tender, 5 to 7 minutes. Transfer to a plate.
5. Sear beef in batches in 3 tbsp oil over medium-high heat. Let the beef brown before turning so the coating gets lightly crisp.
6. Return beef to the skillet, pour in sauce, and simmer 2 to 4 minutes, until glossy and thick enough to coat the beef.
7. Serve orange beef and broccoli over rice. Top with green onions.

## Notes

- What might need testing: Whether the sauce should be sweeter or more orange-forward.
- Possible substitutions: Chicken thighs can work, but cook to 165 degrees F.
- Prep-ahead ideas: Slice beef and broccoli Sunday night. Keep sauce ingredients measured but do not add arrowroot to sauce.
- Family preference concerns: Keep broccoli crisp-tender so the meal still feels quick and fresh.

recipes.title: BBQ Chicken Flatbreads
recipes.cuisine: American BBQ, smoky, cheesy, tangy
recipes.perishabilityNotes: Chicken, cheese, and flatbreads hold well for late week if kept cold.

steps.text: Heat oven to 425 degrees F. Season chicken with salt, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, and black pepper.
Cook chicken in oil in a skillet over medium-high heat until browned and cooked to 165 degrees F, 5 to 7 minutes per side depending on thickness. Rest 5 minutes, then chop or shred.
Toss chicken with 1/2 cup BBQ sauce.
Toss cabbage with apple cider vinegar, brown sugar, and salt. Set aside for topping after baking.
Place flatbreads on sheet pans. Spread each with BBQ sauce, then top with mozzarella, cheddar, BBQ chicken, red onion, and bell pepper.
Bake 8 to 12 minutes, until the cheese melts and the flatbread edges are crisp.
Top with quick cabbage and green onions. Slice and serve.

notes.testing: Flatbread crispness and whether cabbage topping should stay or be replaced with pickled onions.
notes.substitutions: Rotisserie chicken can make this a very fast Saturday meal.
notes.prepAhead: Cook and shred chicken Friday or Saturday morning. Assemble flatbreads only right before baking.
notes.familyPreferenceConcerns: Use less BBQ sauce under the cheese if anyone dislikes a sweet pizza-style base.

## Basic Instructions

1. Heat oven to 425 degrees F. Season chicken with salt, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, and black pepper.
2. Cook chicken in oil in a skillet over medium-high heat until browned and cooked to 165 degrees F, 5 to 7 minutes per side depending on thickness. Rest 5 minutes, then chop or shred.
3. Toss chicken with 1/2 cup BBQ sauce.
4. Toss cabbage with apple cider vinegar, brown sugar, and salt. Set aside for topping after baking.
5. Place flatbreads on sheet pans. Spread each with BBQ sauce, then top with mozzarella, cheddar, BBQ chicken, red onion, and bell pepper.
6. Bake 8 to 12 minutes, until the cheese melts and the flatbread edges are crisp.
7. Top with quick cabbage and green onions. Slice and serve.

## Notes

- What might need testing: Flatbread crispness and whether cabbage topping should stay or be replaced with pickled onions.

# Chicken Cordon Bleu Skillet with Mashed Potatoes

Status: Stage 1 Draft  
Archive folder: `recipe-archive/chicken/`  
Category: Chicken  
Source or inspiration: Week 29 family menu idea  
Date added: 2026-07-04  

## Planning Summary

- Servings: 4
- Estimated prep time: 20 minutes
- Estimated cook time: 35 minutes
- Protein: Chicken breast
- Cuisine or flavor direction: French-inspired chicken, ham, Swiss, creamy Dijon sauce
- Best day to cook: Tuesday
- Perishability notes: Uses chicken, ham, Swiss, cream, and potatoes. Best early-midweek.
- Difficulty: Moderate

## Equipment

- Large skillet
- Medium saucepan or pot
- Potato masher
- Cutting board and knife
- Instant-read thermometer

## Ingredients

Assume nothing is on hand. Include quantities for everything used.

| Quantity | Ingredient | Preferred version/type | Acceptable alternatives | Notes |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 2 lb | Boneless skinless chicken breasts | Cut into cutlets or chunks | Chicken thighs | Main protein |
| 1 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt, use less | Chicken |
| 1/2 tsp | Black pepper | Freshly ground | Ground black pepper | Chicken |
| 1 tsp | Garlic powder | Ground | 2 cloves fresh garlic | Chicken |
| 2 tbsp | Butter | Unsalted | Salted, reduce added salt | Searing |
| 1 tbsp | Neutral oil | Avocado or canola oil | Vegetable oil | Searing |
| 8 oz | Deli ham | Thin sliced, chopped | Black forest ham | Skillet |
| 8 oz | Swiss cheese | Sliced or shredded | Gruyere or provolone | Finish |
| 1 cup | Low-sodium chicken broth | Carton | Chicken stock | Sauce |
| 1 cup | Heavy cream | Heavy whipping cream | Half-and-half | Sauce |
| 1 tbsp | Dijon mustard | Smooth Dijon | Whole grain mustard | Sauce |
| 1 tbsp | Arrowroot powder | Arrowroot starch | Tapioca starch | Sauce thickener |
| 1 tbsp | Lemon juice | Fresh | Bottled lemon juice | Sauce brightness |
| 1 tbsp | Fresh parsley | Chopped | Dried parsley | Finish |
| 3 lb | Yukon gold potatoes | Peeled or unpeeled | Russet potatoes | Mashed potatoes |
| 4 tbsp | Butter | Unsalted | Salted, reduce salt | Potatoes |
| 3/4 cup | Milk | Whole or 2 percent | Half-and-half | Potatoes |
| 1 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt, use less | Potatoes |

## Basic Instructions

1. Start potatoes: cut potatoes into chunks, cover with cold salted water, and boil until fork-tender, 15 to 20 minutes.
2. Season chicken with salt, pepper, and garlic powder. Sear in butter and oil over medium-high heat until browned and cooked to 165 degrees F. Transfer to a plate.
3. Add ham to the skillet and cook 1 to 2 minutes, just to warm and lightly brown.
4. Whisk broth, cream, Dijon, arrowroot, and lemon juice in a bowl. Pour into the skillet and simmer 3 to 5 minutes, scraping browned bits, until lightly thickened.
5. Return chicken to the skillet. Top with Swiss cheese, cover, and cook on low until the cheese melts and the sauce coats the chicken.
6. Drain potatoes and mash with butter, milk, and salt. Add more milk a splash at a time if needed.
7. Serve chicken and ham cream sauce over mashed potatoes. Finish with parsley.

## Notes

- What might need testing: Sauce thickness and whether the family wants more Dijon.
- Possible substitutions: Serve over rice if potatoes feel like too much work.
- Prep-ahead ideas: Cut chicken and ham Tuesday morning. Do not make mashed potatoes ahead for this test.
- Family preference concerns: Swiss can be assertive; provolone is a milder backup.

# Chicken Tikka Masala (Weeknight Version)

Status: Stage 1 Draft  
Archive folder: `recipe-archive/chicken/`  
Category: Chicken  
Source or inspiration: Previous family draft recipe  
Date added: 2026-07-04  

## Planning Summary

- Servings: 4
- Estimated prep time: 15 minutes
- Estimated cook time: 30 minutes
- Protein: Chicken thighs
- Cuisine or flavor direction: Indian-inspired, creamy tomato, garam masala, weeknight-friendly
- Best day to cook: Early or midweek
- Perishability notes: Uses fresh chicken, onion, garlic, ginger, and coconut milk. Cook chicken within 1 to 2 days of shopping.

## Equipment

- Large skillet or Dutch oven
- Rice cooker or saucepan
- Cutting board and knife
- Small bowl

## Ingredients

Assume nothing is on hand. Include quantities for everything used.

| Quantity | Ingredient | Preferred version/type | Acceptable alternatives | Notes |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 2 lb | Boneless skinless chicken thighs | Trimmed, cut into bite-size pieces | Chicken breast, watched carefully | Main protein |
| 1 tbsp | Neutral oil | Avocado or canola oil | Vegetable oil | Browning |
| 1 | Yellow onion | Medium, diced | White onion | Sauce base |
| 4 cloves | Garlic | Fresh minced | 1 tsp garlic powder | Sauce |
| 1 tbsp | Fresh ginger | Grated | 1 tsp ground ginger | Sauce |
| 2 tbsp | Tomato paste | Canned or tube | 1/2 cup crushed tomatoes | Sauce |
| 1 can, 13.5 oz | Coconut milk | Full-fat canned | Light coconut milk | Sauce |
| 1 tsp | Paprika | Sweet | Smoked paprika | Sauce |
| 2 tsp | Garam masala | Ground spice blend | Curry powder as backup | Key flavor |
| 1 tsp | Ground cumin | Ground | None | Sauce |
| 1 tsp | Ground turmeric | Ground | Skip if unavailable | Sauce |
| 1/2 tsp | Cayenne pepper | Ground | Skip for mild | Optional heat |
| 1 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt, use less | Sauce |
| 1 tbsp | Arrowroot powder | Arrowroot starch | Tapioca starch | Optional thickener |
| 2 tbsp | Water | Cold | Chicken broth | Slurry |
| 1 1/2 cups dry | Jasmine rice | Long-grain jasmine | Basmati rice | Serving |
| 2 1/4 cups | Water | Cold | Low-sodium broth | Rice |
| 1/2 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Rice |

## Basic Instructions

1. Start rice by rinsing jasmine rice, then simmering with water and salt for 15 minutes. Rest covered 10 minutes.
2. Heat oil in a large skillet or Dutch oven. Brown chicken in batches until lightly browned, then transfer to a plate.
3. Cook onion until softened, 4 to 6 minutes. Add garlic and ginger and cook 30 seconds.
4. Stir in tomato paste, paprika, garam masala, cumin, turmeric, cayenne, and salt. Cook 1 minute.
5. Add coconut milk and browned chicken. Simmer 18 to 22 minutes, until chicken reaches 165 degrees F and sauce is flavorful.
6. If a thicker sauce is desired, mix arrowroot with cold water and stir into the simmering sauce for 1 to 2 minutes.
7. Serve over rice.

## Notes

- What might need testing: Sauce thickness and heat level.
- Possible substitutions: Heavy cream can replace coconut milk for a richer non-coconut version.
- Prep-ahead ideas: Dice onion and chicken the night before; keep separate.
- Family preference concerns: Start without cayenne for a family-friendly baseline.

# Peruvian Chicken with Green Sauce, Roasted Potatoes, and Simple Salad

Status: Stage 1 Draft  
Archive folder: `recipe-archive/chicken/`  
Category: Chicken  
Source or inspiration: Week 29 family menu idea  
Date added: 2026-07-04  

## Planning Summary

- Servings: 6
- Estimated prep time: 25 minutes, plus marinade time
- Estimated cook time: 45 minutes
- Protein: Chicken
- Cuisine or flavor direction: Peruvian-inspired pollo a la brasa, smoky, garlicky, citrusy, herb sauce
- Best day to cook: Sunday
- Perishability notes: Uses fresh chicken, cilantro, jalapeno, lettuce, tomatoes, and yogurt. Best early in the week.
- Difficulty: Signature

## Equipment

- Grill, grill pan, or sheet pan
- Blender or food processor
- Large mixing bowl or zip-top bag
- Sheet pan
- Instant-read thermometer

## Ingredients

Assume nothing is on hand. Include quantities for everything used.

| Quantity | Ingredient | Preferred version/type | Acceptable alternatives | Notes |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 6 lb | Bone-in chicken leg quarters or thighs | Skin-on | Boneless thighs, reduce cook time | Main protein |
| 3 tbsp | Neutral oil | Avocado or canola oil | Vegetable oil | Marinade |
| 1/4 cup | Lime juice | Fresh | Bottled lime juice | Marinade |
| 8 cloves | Garlic | Fresh minced | 2 tsp garlic powder | Marinade and sauce |
| 2 tbsp | Low-sodium soy sauce | Low-sodium | Tamari | Marinade depth |
| 2 tsp | Ground cumin | Ground | None | Marinade |
| 2 tsp | Smoked paprika | Smoked | Sweet paprika | Marinade |
| 2 tsp | Dried oregano | Dried | 2 tbsp fresh oregano | Marinade |
| 2 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt, use less | Marinade |
| 1 tsp | Black pepper | Freshly ground | Ground black pepper | Marinade |
| 3 lb | Yukon gold potatoes | Cut into wedges | Russet potatoes | Side |
| 2 tbsp | Neutral oil | Avocado or canola oil | Vegetable oil | Potatoes |
| 1 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Potatoes |
| 1/2 tsp | Black pepper | Ground | Freshly ground | Potatoes |
| 1 cup | Cilantro | Fresh packed leaves and tender stems | Parsley | Green sauce |
| 1 | Jalapeno | Seeded for mild | Serrano for hotter | Green sauce |
| 1/2 cup | Mayonnaise | Regular | Avocado-oil mayo | Green sauce body |
| 1/2 cup | Plain Greek yogurt | Full-fat or 2 percent | Sour cream | Green sauce tang |
| 2 tbsp | Lime juice | Fresh | Bottled lime juice | Green sauce |
| 1/2 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Green sauce |
| 2 hearts | Romaine lettuce | Chopped | Green leaf lettuce | Salad |
| 1 pint | Grape tomatoes | Halved | Cherry tomatoes | Salad |
| 1/2 | Red onion | Thinly sliced | Shallot | Salad |
| 1 tbsp | Olive oil | Extra-virgin or regular | Avocado oil | Salad |
| 1 tbsp | Lime juice | Fresh | Lemon juice | Salad |
| 1/4 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Salad |

## Basic Instructions

1. Mix oil, lime juice, garlic, soy sauce, cumin, smoked paprika, oregano, salt, and pepper. Coat chicken thoroughly and refrigerate at least 2 hours, or up to overnight.
2. Heat grill to medium, or heat oven to 425 degrees F if roasting. Start potatoes first by tossing wedges with oil, salt, and pepper. Roast 35 to 45 minutes, turning once, until browned and tender.
3. Grill chicken skin-side down over medium heat until browned, then move to indirect heat and cook until the thickest pieces reach 165 degrees F. If roasting, place chicken on a sheet pan and roast 40 to 50 minutes.
4. Blend cilantro, jalapeno, mayonnaise, yogurt, garlic, lime juice, and salt until smooth. Taste and adjust lime or salt.
5. Toss romaine, tomatoes, and red onion with olive oil, lime juice, and salt right before serving.
6. Serve chicken with roasted potatoes, salad, and plenty of green sauce.

## Notes

- What might need testing: Green sauce heat level and whether the family prefers yogurt-heavy or mayo-heavy sauce.
- Possible substitutions: Boneless thighs work for faster cooking, but check early and pull at 165 degrees F.
- Prep-ahead ideas: Make marinade and green sauce Sunday morning. Roast potatoes day-of for best texture.
- Family preference concerns: Keep some sauce mild by blending without jalapeno first, then adding jalapeno to the rest.

# Thai Peanut Stir Fry

Status: Stage 1 Draft  
Archive folder: `recipe-archive/chicken/`  
Category: Chicken  
Source or inspiration: Previous family draft recipe  
Date added: 2026-07-04  

## Planning Summary

- Servings: 4
- Estimated prep time: 20 minutes
- Estimated cook time: 20 minutes
- Protein: Chicken or tofu
- Cuisine or flavor direction: Thai-inspired peanut sauce, savory-sweet, ginger-garlic
- Best day to cook: Midweek
- Perishability notes: Uses chicken or tofu plus broccoli, peppers, and carrots. Cook fresh vegetables before they soften.

## Equipment

- Wok or large skillet
- Mixing bowl
- Cutting board and knife
- Spoon or spatula

## Ingredients

Assume nothing is on hand. Include quantities for everything used.

| Quantity | Ingredient | Preferred version/type | Acceptable alternatives | Notes |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 2 lb | Chicken breast or tofu | Chicken sliced thin, extra-firm tofu pressed | Chicken thighs | Protein |
| 2 tbsp | Neutral oil | Avocado or canola oil | Vegetable oil | Stir fry |
| 1 lb | Broccoli florets | Fresh | Frozen broccoli florets | Vegetable |
| 2 | Bell peppers | Red, yellow, or orange, sliced | Extra broccoli | Vegetable |
| 2 | Carrots | Thinly sliced | Matchstick carrots | Vegetable |
| 1/2 cup | Peanut butter | Creamy | Crunchy peanut butter | Sauce |
| 1/4 cup | Low-sodium soy sauce | Low-sodium | Tamari | Sauce |
| 2 tbsp | Honey | Liquid honey | Brown sugar | Sauce |
| 2 tbsp | Rice vinegar | Unseasoned | Lime juice | Sauce |
| 3 cloves | Garlic | Fresh minced | 1 tsp garlic powder | Sauce |
| 1 tbsp | Fresh ginger | Grated | 1 tsp ground ginger | Sauce |
| 1 tbsp | Sesame oil | Toasted preferred | Neutral oil | Sauce |
| 1 tbsp | Arrowroot powder | Arrowroot starch | Tapioca starch | Slurry |
| 1/2 cup | Water | Warm | Coconut milk | Sauce thinning |
| 1 1/2 cups dry | Jasmine rice | Long-grain jasmine | Long-grain white rice | Serving |

## Basic Instructions

1. Cook rice according to package directions if serving with rice.
2. Whisk peanut butter, soy sauce, honey, rice vinegar, garlic, ginger, sesame oil, arrowroot, and warm water until smooth.
3. Heat oil in a wok. Cook chicken until browned and 165 degrees F, or cook tofu until golden. Transfer to a plate.
4. Stir fry broccoli, bell peppers, and carrots until crisp-tender.
5. Return protein to the pan. Add peanut sauce and simmer 2 to 4 minutes, until thickened and coating everything.
6. Serve over rice.

## Planning Summary

- Cuisine or flavor direction: Lettuce wraps, soy-ginger, savory

- Saucepan
- Skillet

## Ingredients

Assume nothing is on hand. Include quantities for everything used.

| Quantity | Ingredient | Preferred version/type | Acceptable alternatives | Notes |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1 cup | Red lentils | Dry red lentils | Brown lentils, longer cook time | Base |
| 2 cups | Broth | Low-sodium chicken or vegetable broth | Water plus salt | Lentils |
| 1 lb | Ground beef | 85/15 or 90/10 | Skip for vegetarian | Optional protein |
| 1 | Yellow onion | Diced | White onion | Filling |
| 3 cloves | Garlic | Fresh minced | 1 tsp garlic powder | Filling |
| 2 | Carrots | Finely diced or shredded | Matchstick carrots | Filling |
| 1 tbsp | Fresh ginger | Grated | 1 tsp ground ginger | Filling |
| 1/4 cup | Low-sodium soy sauce | Low-sodium | Liquid aminos or tamari | Sauce |
| 1 tbsp | Sesame oil | Toasted preferred | Neutral oil | Sauce |
| 1 tbsp | Neutral oil | Avocado or canola oil | Vegetable oil | Cooking |
| 1 head | Butter lettuce | Whole leaves | Romaine leaves | Serving |

## Basic Instructions

1. Simmer lentils with broth until soft and thick, 12 to 15 minutes.
2. If using beef, brown it in a skillet and drain excess grease if needed.
3. Add oil, onion, carrots, garlic, and ginger to the skillet. Cook until vegetables soften, 4 to 6 minutes.

- What might need testing: Whether the family prefers lentil-only or beef-lentil filling.

# Vietnamese Lemongrass Pork Bowls with Pickled Vegetables and Herbs

Status: Stage 1 Draft  
Archive folder: `recipe-archive/pork/`  
Category: Pork  
Source or inspiration: Week 29 family menu idea  
Date added: 2026-07-04  

## Planning Summary

- Servings: 4
- Estimated prep time: 25 minutes, plus quick pickle time
- Estimated cook time: 20 minutes
- Protein: Pork chops
- Cuisine or flavor direction: Vietnamese-inspired lemongrass, fish sauce, lime, herbs
- Best day to cook: Wednesday
- Perishability notes: Uses fresh pork, herbs, cucumber, carrots, and radishes. Best midweek.
- Difficulty: Easy

## Equipment

- Large skillet or grill pan
- Medium saucepan with lid
- Mixing bowls
- Cutting board and knife
- Jar or container for pickles

## Ingredients

Assume nothing is on hand. Include quantities for everything used.

| Quantity | Ingredient | Preferred version/type | Acceptable alternatives | Notes |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1 1/2 lb | Pork chops | Boneless, thin-cut or sliced thin | Pork tenderloin | Main protein |
| 3 stalks | Lemongrass | Tender inner portion minced | 2 tbsp lemongrass paste | Marinade |
| 4 cloves | Garlic | Fresh minced | 1 tsp garlic powder | Marinade |
| 1 tbsp | Fresh ginger | Grated | 1 tsp ground ginger | Marinade |
| 3 tbsp | Fish sauce | Good-quality | Extra soy sauce | Marinade |
| 2 tbsp | Lime juice | Fresh | Bottled lime juice | Marinade |
| 2 tbsp | Brown sugar | Light brown sugar | Honey | Marinade |
| 1 tbsp | Neutral oil | Avocado or canola oil | Vegetable oil | Marinade |
| 1/2 tsp | Ground coriander | Ground | Skip if unavailable | Marinade |
| 1/2 tsp | Black pepper | Ground | Freshly ground | Marinade |
| 1 1/2 cups | Jasmine rice | Dry | Long-grain white rice | Bowl base |
| 2 1/4 cups | Water | Cold water | Low-sodium broth | Rice |
| 1/2 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Rice |
| 2 large | Carrots | Cut into matchsticks | Bagged matchstick carrots | Pickles |
| 1 | English cucumber | Thinly sliced | 2 Persian cucumbers | Pickles and bowls |
| 1 bunch | Radishes | Thinly sliced | Extra cucumber | Pickles |
| 1/2 cup | Rice vinegar | Unseasoned | White vinegar | Pickles |
| 2 tbsp | Granulated sugar | White sugar | Honey | Pickles |
| 1 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt, use less | Pickles |
| 1/2 cup | Cilantro | Fresh leaves | Parsley | Finish |
| 1/2 cup | Fresh mint | Fresh leaves | Basil | Finish |
| 1/2 cup | Fresh basil | Fresh leaves | Mint | Finish |
| 1 | Lime | Cut into wedges | Bottled lime juice | Serving |

## Basic Instructions

1. Mix lemongrass, garlic, ginger, fish sauce, lime juice, brown sugar, oil, coriander, and black pepper. Coat pork and marinate 20 minutes, or up to 8 hours.
2. Start rice: rinse jasmine rice, then simmer with water and salt for 15 minutes. Rest covered 10 minutes.
3. Make quick pickles by stirring rice vinegar, sugar, and salt until dissolved. Add carrots, cucumber, and radishes. Let sit at least 20 minutes.
4. Heat a skillet or grill pan over medium-high heat. Cook pork in a thin layer until browned and cooked through, 2 to 4 minutes per side depending on thickness.
5. Slice pork if needed. Build bowls with rice, pork, drained pickled vegetables, herbs, and lime wedges.

## Notes

- What might need testing: Lemongrass strength and pickle sweetness.
- Possible substitutions: Pork tenderloin can be sliced thin and cooked quickly.

## Basic Instructions

1. Heat oven to 400 degrees F. Place salmon on a baking sheet and rub with olive oil, half the salt, and half the pepper.
2. Bake salmon 12 to 15 minutes, until it flakes easily.
3. Cook rice according to package directions.
4. Mix Greek yogurt, lemon juice, garlic, remaining salt, and remaining pepper.
5. Prepare cucumber, tomatoes, red onion, feta, and olives.

# Harvest Grain Bowl

Status: Stage 1 Draft  
Archive folder: `recipe-archive/sides/`  
Category: Vegetarian  
Source or inspiration: Previous family draft recipe  
Date added: 2026-07-04  

## Planning Summary

- Servings: 4
- Estimated prep time: 20 minutes
- Estimated cook time: 35 minutes
- Protein: Pumpkin seeds plus optional chicken
- Cuisine or flavor direction: Fall grain bowl, roasted vegetables, maple Dijon dressing
- Best day to cook: Any weeknight
- Perishability notes: Uses sweet potatoes, Brussels sprouts, and spinach. Best while spinach is fresh.

## Equipment

- Baking sheet
- Saucepan
- Mixing bowl
- Cutting board and knife

## Ingredients

Assume nothing is on hand. Include quantities for everything used.

| Quantity | Ingredient | Preferred version/type | Acceptable alternatives | Notes |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1 1/2 cups dry | Brown rice or quinoa | Brown rice for heartier bowl, quinoa for faster cooking | Farro | Grain base |
| 2 large | Sweet potatoes | Peeled or scrubbed, cubed | Butternut squash | Roasted vegetable |
| 1 lb | Brussels sprouts | Trimmed and halved | Broccoli florets | Roasted vegetable |
| 4 cups | Spinach | Fresh baby spinach | Mixed greens | Bowl |
| 1/2 cup | Pumpkin seeds | Roasted pepitas | Sunflower seeds | Crunch |
| 1/3 cup | Dried cranberries | Sweetened dried cranberries | Raisins | Sweetness |
| 3 tbsp | Olive oil | Extra-virgin or regular | Avocado oil | Roasting and dressing |
| 1 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt, use less | Divided |
| 1/2 tsp | Black pepper | Ground | Freshly ground | Vegetables |
| 1 tbsp | Dijon mustard | Smooth Dijon | Whole grain mustard | Dressing |
| 1 tbsp | Maple syrup | Pure maple syrup | Honey | Dressing |
| 1 tbsp | Apple cider vinegar | Apple cider vinegar | Lemon juice | Dressing |

## Basic Instructions

1. Heat oven to 425 degrees F. Toss sweet potatoes and Brussels sprouts with 2 tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper.
2. Roast 25 to 35 minutes, stirring once, until browned and tender.
3. Cook brown rice or quinoa according to package directions.
4. Whisk 1 tbsp olive oil, Dijon, maple syrup, apple cider vinegar, and a pinch of salt.
5. Assemble bowls with grain, spinach, roasted vegetables, pumpkin seeds, dried cranberries, and dressing.

## Notes

- What might need testing: Whether this needs added chicken to be filling enough.
- Possible substitutions: Add grilled chicken, turkey meatballs, or salmon for more protein.
- Prep-ahead ideas: Cook grain and roast vegetables ahead; dress when serving.
- Family preference concerns: Dried cranberries make it sweeter; reduce if needed.

# Mediterranean Turkey Meatballs with Lemon Rice, Tzatziki, and Roasted Vegetables

Status: Stage 1 Draft  
Archive folder: `recipe-archive/turkey/`  
Category: Turkey  
Source or inspiration: Week 29 family menu idea  
Date added: 2026-07-04  

## Planning Summary

- Servings: 4
- Estimated prep time: 30 minutes
- Estimated cook time: 35 minutes
- Protein: Ground turkey
- Cuisine or flavor direction: Greek/Mediterranean, lemon, herbs, yogurt sauce
- Best day to cook: Thursday
- Perishability notes: Uses ground turkey, yogurt, cucumber, dill, broccoli, peppers, and zucchini. Best mid-late week.
- Difficulty: Moderate

## Equipment

- Sheet pan
- Medium saucepan with lid
- Mixing bowls
- Box grater
- Cutting board and knife

## Ingredients

Assume nothing is on hand. Include quantities for everything used.

| Quantity | Ingredient | Preferred version/type | Acceptable alternatives | Notes |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1 1/2 lb | Ground turkey | 93/7 preferred | Ground chicken | Meatballs |
| 1 large | Egg | Large | 2 tbsp Greek yogurt | Binder |
| 1/2 cup | Panko breadcrumbs | Plain | Regular breadcrumbs | Binder |
| 1/3 cup | Feta cheese | Crumbled | Skip if needed | Meatballs |
| 3 cloves | Garlic | Fresh minced | 1 tsp garlic powder | Meatballs and tzatziki |
| 1 tbsp | Lemon zest | Fresh | Skip if unavailable | Meatballs |
| 2 tbsp | Lemon juice | Fresh | Bottled lemon juice | Meatballs and rice |
| 2 tbsp | Fresh parsley | Chopped | Dried parsley | Meatballs |
| 1 tbsp | Fresh dill | Chopped | 1 tsp dried dill | Meatballs |
| 1 tsp | Dried oregano | Greek oregano preferred | Italian oregano | Meatballs |
| 1 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt, use less | Meatballs |
| 1/2 tsp | Black pepper | Ground | Freshly ground | Meatballs |
| 1 1/2 cups | Jasmine rice | Dry | Long-grain white rice | Lemon rice |
| 2 1/4 cups | Water | Cold water | Low-sodium broth | Lemon rice |
| 1 tbsp | Olive oil | Extra-virgin or regular | Avocado oil | Lemon rice |
| 1 tbsp | Lemon juice | Fresh | Bottled lemon juice | Lemon rice |
| 1/2 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Lemon rice |
| 1 1/2 lb | Broccoli florets | Fresh | Frozen broccoli florets | Roasted vegetables |
| 2 | Red bell peppers | Sliced | Orange or yellow peppers | Roasted vegetables |
| 2 | Zucchini | Sliced | Extra bell pepper | Roasted vegetables |
| 2 tbsp | Olive oil | Extra-virgin or regular | Avocado oil | Roasted vegetables |
| 1 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt, use less | Roasted vegetables |
| 1/2 tsp | Black pepper | Ground | Freshly ground | Roasted vegetables |
| 1 cup | Plain Greek yogurt | Full-fat or 2 percent | Plain yogurt, strained | Tzatziki |
| 1/2 | English cucumber | Grated and squeezed dry | Persian cucumber | Tzatziki |
| 1 tbsp | Fresh dill | Chopped | 1 tsp dried dill | Tzatziki |
| 1 tbsp | Lemon juice | Fresh | Bottled lemon juice | Tzatziki |
| 1/2 tsp | Kosher salt | Kosher | Fine sea salt | Tzatziki |

## Basic Instructions

1. Heat oven to 425 degrees F. Toss broccoli, bell peppers, and zucchini with olive oil, salt, and pepper on a sheet pan.
2. Mix turkey, egg, panko, feta, garlic, lemon zest, lemon juice, parsley, dill, oregano, salt, and pepper. Shape into 18 to 22 meatballs.
3. Place meatballs on the sheet pan with vegetables or on a second pan. Roast 18 to 22 minutes, until meatballs reach 165 degrees F and vegetables are browned.
4. Cook rice with water, olive oil, and salt. Rest covered 10 minutes, then fluff with lemon juice.
5. Make tzatziki by mixing Greek yogurt, squeezed cucumber, garlic, dill, lemon juice, and salt.
6. Serve meatballs over lemon rice with roasted vegetables and tzatziki.

## Notes

- What might need testing: Turkey meatball moisture. Add an extra spoonful of yogurt if the mixture seems dry.
- Possible substitutions: Ground chicken works, but may be softer to shape.
- Prep-ahead ideas: Make tzatziki Wednesday night. Shape meatballs Thursday morning if cooking that evening.
- Family preference concerns: Keep feta moderate so the meatballs do not turn too salty.
